title: Googles Gemini Spark is now available to more users
body: Google's Gemini Spark , the company's AI agent that completes tasks and monitors stuff in the background for you, is now more widely available. You still can't get it for free, though. Starting today, Gemini Spark is available for users in the U.S. who are paying for the $20/month Google Ai Pro tier. Globally, you'll need to pay for Google AI Ultra, which starts at about $100 (depending on the market). The one exception to this are folks in the European Economic Area (EEA), the UK, Switzerland, or Nigeria, which aren't (yet) getting access to Gemini Spark. Google's support documents say you shouldn't worry if you don't have access to Spark yet, as the company is "rapidly expanding access to more users and businesses over the coming weeks." SEE ALSO: Google releases two new Gemini models, but still no Gemini 3.5 Pro Gemini Spark is based on Gemini 3.5 and, like other similar agents, is designed to work in the background. Offering a daily summary of your tasks for the day, based on your calendar, documents, notes, and inbox, is one example of what it can do . Competitors offer a similar agent, and for a similar price. OpenAI's ChatGPT Work is only available on paid tiers, as is Anthropic's Claude Cowork . Pricing for both starts at about $20 per month, though the difference is that they're available in Europe and the UK.

title: The fastest U.S. mobile and internet services of 2026: How did your provider do?
body: Telecommunication analytics company Ookla has released its U.S. Speedtest Connectivity Report for the first half of 2026, evaluating how internet service providers have performed thus far. T-Mobile came out on top, sweeping the mobile category, while AT T Fiber and Verizon both picked up awards for fixed internet. SEE ALSO: T-Mobile will automatically upgrade some legacy phone plans to higher-price plans Ookla's report crowned T-Mobile the best mobile network and best 5G network in the U.S. overall, with a median download speed of 275.55 Mbps (or 314.38 Mbps on 5G). According to Ookla's analysis, T-Mobile offered the fastest mobile speeds across 47 U.S. states from January to June this year. The exceptions were North Dakota and Vermont, who had no winners, and Alaska, where GCI came out on top for mobile speeds. T-Mobile also received the highest experience scores across mobile and specifically 5G networks, including when specifically used for gaming or streaming video, and was found to be the most consistent network as well. Coverage is the only category where T-Mobile didn't come in first, with Verizon and AT T taking the top two spots instead. Even so, T-Mobile was the clear winner of the mobile category. While Ookla's rankings primarily focus on performance and don't take pricing or value into account, T-Mobile also had the highest consumer sentiment rating. Speedtest users gave T-Mobile an average of 3.77 out of five stars, while Verizon and AT T scored 3.25 and 3.09 respectively. Credit: Ookla Meanwhile, the fixed internet category belonged to AT T Fiber. Ookla crowne

title: Silo Season 3, episode 4s ending explained: What happened to Bernard?
body: Every time somebody journeys down to the depths of the silo, they seem to find something mysterious. Last season, it was the robotic voice of the algorithm . This season, it's Bernard (Tim Robbins). But hang on a minute, isn't Bernard dead? Well, that's what we were led to believe back in Silo Season 3, episode 1. But it looks like there may be more to the story... SEE ALSO: 'Silo' Season 3, episode 3: Who is the mysterious gray-haired man? What happens at the end of Silo Season 3, episode 4? Juliette (Rebecca Ferguson) spends most of episode 4 on the run after Camille (Alexandria Riley) tries to have her murdered in her hospital bed. With the help of her nurse (Jacqueline Berces), Juliette manages to escape, only to run into Sims (Common) waiting for her in the pump room. She runs away before he can tell her what he wants to say, and with the help of her friends in mechanical, she finds her way back down to the very bottom of the silo, where Lukas Kyle (Avi Nash) discovered the tunnel in Season 2 . Lukas isn't down there, though. Instead, Juliette hears a person coughing, pulls back a curtain, and sees her former nemesis, Bernard. He's badly burned, and lying in a bed. "Hello, Juliette," he rasps. How is Bernard still alive? Okay, so a quick recap: Back at the end of Season 2 , Bernard and Juliette were trapped in the airlock together while the anti-contamination fire burned its way through the confined space. Juliette was wearing a modified fire suit; Bernard wasn't. He managed to throw himself to the ground pretty quickly just before the fire started to burn, but his sur

title: Silo Season 3, episode 4: Is Sims actually on Juliettes side?
body: In the world of Silo , allegiances are constantly shifting, and you can never be 100 percent sure who to trust. Sims (Common), however, has been a fairly consistent bad guy throughout and there's never been any indication that he's anything but an enemy to Juliette (Rebecca Ferguson). Until now? In Season 3, episode 4, there are a couple of moments that have led to us wondering if Sims might be slowly changing allegiances. So, what clues are there, and what could they mean? SEE ALSO: 'Silo' Season 3, episode 3: Who is the mysterious gray-haired man? What happens with Sims and Juliette in Season 3, episode 4? Juliette starts off episode 4 in a tough spot. She's trapped in the silo's medical bay, and Camille (Alexandria Riley) has dispatched a trusted soldier to murder her in cold blood. The good news? Her nurse (Jacqueline Berces), who we previously thought was working against her, is actually Team Juliette. "I'm the reason you started getting your memory back. I took you off the forgetting drugs," says the nurse, after she's saved Juliette's life and is helping her escape. "No. I spit them out," Juliette replies. "Even before that, somebody asked me to replace them," the nurse responds. She then causes a distraction that allows Juliette to evade the raiders, but not before telling her to go to the pump room on level 76. And who does Juliette find when she eventually makes it to the pump room in question? title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 24, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. title: Moon phase today: What the Moon will look like on July 24
body: The Moon is even closer to full tonight, as the Waxing Gibbous phase continues and more of its surface is lit by the Sun. What is today s Moon phase? As of Friday, July 24, NASA's Daily Moon Guide tracker tells us the Moon phase is in Waxing Gibbous phase, with 68% of its surface visible. You won't need any visual aids to be able to spot the Mares Fecunditatis and Tranquillitatis, as well as the Tycho Crater. Binoculars will also help you to see the Mare Nectaris, Alphonsus Crater, and the Alps Mountains. A telescope helps you see much more, including the Apollo 11, 16, and 17 landing spots. When is the next Full Moon? The next Full Moon will take place on July 29. What are Moon phases? The Moon takes roughly 29.5 days to complete its journey around Earth, passing through eight distinct phases during each lunar cycle, according to NASA . While the Moon s same side is always visible from our planet, the portion of its surface lit by the Sun changes as it moves along its orbit. These shifting levels of illumination are what create the different appearances we see throughout the month, from a slender crescent shape to a half-lit Moon and finally a bright Full Moon, before the cycle begins once again. The eight phases are: New Moon - The Moon is between Earth and the sun, so the side we see is dark (in other words, it's invisible to the eye). Waxing Crescent - A small sliver of light appears on the right side (Northern Hemisphere). First Quarter - Half of the Moon is lit on the right side. It looks like a half-Moon. Waxing Gibbous - More than half is lit up, but it s not quite

title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 24, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 24, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 24, 2026
body: Today's Wordle answer should be easy to solve if you're always put together. If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 24, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? body: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update , which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to the correct device. title: Instagram is coming for creepy Meta glasses videos
body: Meta s smart glasses allow creators to record strangers without holding up an obvious camera. Unsurprisingly, it's enabled a lot of bad behavior . Now, Instagram is placing new limits on what happens to that footage once it reaches the platform. In an Instagram Story (first reported by Business Insider ), Instagram head Adam Mosseri said the platform would remove smart-glasses videos that show people being harassed or taken advantage of. "If you re posting content that is taking advantage of people and harassing them, like a lot of these pickup line kind of videos that we ve heard of and seen, then we re going to take the content down," Mosseri reportedly said. "We don t want people to be surreptitiously taking videos of other people and harassing them and then posting them on our platform," he added. The belated crackdown covers formats including pickup videos in which creators approach people who may not realize they are being recorded and prank videos targeting cashiers, fast-food employees, and other workers. The report also noted Instagram had deactivated two pickup-artist accounts that used smart glasses to film women in public, both with more than 1 million followers. Meta confirmed the accounts were removed for violating its policy against harassing content, but did not say when the removals occurred. Meta also didn't explain how Instagram will determine when an interaction becomes harassment, or how many posts and accounts have been removed. But recent incidents show why the platform is under pressure to draw a clearer line and how much damage can be done before a

title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
author: Scharon Harding
body: LG is reportedly pulling a McAfee pop-up ad from a controversial app that some of its monitors have stealthily installed on connected computers. An example of the pop-up ad is seen in a still from Gamers Nexus' video. Credit: Gamers Nexus/Windows/YouTube The app, LG Monitor App Installer, installs itself through a Windows Update alongside monitor driver updates. LG Monitor App Installer then pushes ads for a 30-day free trial for McAfee, as well as other software, via a pop-up on affected systems, multiple users have reported. This has drawn negative attention recently, with complaints surfacing on Reddit and a subsequent video from YouTube channel Gamers Nexus . In the video, editor-in-chief Steve Burke said that the publication paid $1,200 for an LG UltraGear 3GX900A-B gaming monitor (the monitor’s price dropped to $600 two days later, Burke said) for testing and replicated the behavior “several times” across “multiple” Windows 11 systems. Read full article Comments

body: Echo Show's 21 attached to the adjustable stand, which is sold separately (and also discounted). | Image: Amazon Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319.99 (usually $399.99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. Amazon Echo Show 21 The Echo Show 21 can act as the centerpiece of any burgeoning smart home. The 1080p smart display is a Matter and Thread controller, meaning you can locally control compatible devices using voice commands or a range of widgets. Its built-in auto-framing camera also facilitates video calls. Plus, it has decent speakers for music and other features shared among Alexa displays. Where to Buy: $399.99 $319.99 at Best Buy $399.99 $319.99 at Home Depot The Echo Show 21’s smart home controls are unique to it and the smaller Echo Show 15 that’s just $20 cheaper. They boast support for Zigbee, Matter, and Thread, providing seamless control for numerous devices. The included voice remote can’t interact with widgets or Alexa skills, but you can use it to control your streaming services or other media. There’s a built-in 13-megapixel camera, too, in case you want to video chat with a pal, with auto-framing to keep you centered even if you’re rushing around the house. The panel is designed to mount on the wall, but if you’d rather set it on a counter or bookshelf,

title: Apple could deactivate leased iPhones remotely for non-payment, report says
body: The iPhone 18 is about to launch , along with the final version of its operating system, iOS 27 , most likely in September. But a new report says it may arrive with a feature that lets Apple, and third-party lenders, remotely deactivate phones if customers fall behind on payments. With the iOS 27 beta now widely available, 9to5Mac found code that would let Apple place delinquent iPhones in "Restricted Mode." This would limit users to a short list of widgets and apps, including the App Store, Clock, Settings, Wallet, and Passwords, according to the report. In theory, 9to5Mac says the system could work like this: Authorized financing partners who lease iPhones to customers get to perform regular status checks to see if the contract is still valid. If a customer falls behind, Restricted Mode would be activated. The feature is "unequivocally present" in the iOS 27 beta, according to 9to5Mac and a future update to iOS 26 could bring this feature to older iPhones as well. SEE ALSO: iPhone 18 news, leaks, and rumors: Release date, iPhone 18 Pro details, more info The era of internet-connected "smart" devices has had plenty of benefits for consumers, but as consumer advocates have long warned, it also allows brands to remotely turn off devices . Why might Apple be testing a tool like this right now? Let's dive in. Credit: Timothy Werth / Mashable If you've bought any new tech products recently, you've probably noticed: New devices are getting really expensive . Just this month Apple raised prices on a variety of its products. iPhone prices haven't risen just yet, but Tim Cook has p

title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
author: Beth Mole
body: The Food and Drug Administration announced on Wednesday that it's investigating yet another outbreak of Cyclospora , the unicellular foodborne parasite behind a nationwide surge in explosive watery diarrhea illnesses. The FDA said 72 cases have been identified in this outbreak, but the agency provided no details on the situation. This announcement marks the sixth Cyclospora outbreak investigation the FDA has opened this year. Overall, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has gotten reports of more than 11,500 cases (including 4,173 confirmed and over 7,400 probable) from 41 states. Among the cases, there have been 308 hospitalizations and no deaths. This year's tally so far—just in July—greatly exceeds the normal number of cases the US sees in a whole year. In recent years, the US has reported between 2,000 and 5,000 cases in a year, according to CDC data . With a new outbreak announced, the tally of cases is likely to continue climbing. Nevertheless, when asked about the national surge in cases earlier this week, anti-vaccine Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said the situation was " under control ." Read full article Comments

title: The federal government is erasing bike lanes — from its websites
body: In yet another interesting direction of resources , federal transportation leaders are systematically removing bike safety information from official websites. Transportation employees, including those working for the Federal Highway Administration, have been ordered to find and even remove mentions of bikes , bike lanes, and bike safety from online pages, an investigation by NPR found. In the last week, dozens of safety pages have been taken down or edited to remove public access to resources, the publication reported, such as the Pedestrian and Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool . SEE ALSO: LA Metro tells the public to Ride the D and the internet loves it An anonymous Department of Transportation staffer told NPR that the decision was "asinine" and was not "making the sites better, faster or quicker." When asked about the changes, a department spokesperson wrote:"Since nearly 80% of the population uses cars to get to work and their important medical appointments, we are taking a closer look at federal guidance to ensure American families have the infrastructure they need. This is a common sense approach despite the Left's belief that everyone should ride a bike to their shoebox-sized apartment to eat crickets to achieve a phony climate agenda." The website edits come shortly after remarks by Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy, who referred to Biden-era transportation initiatives as DEI expenditures. "I just redirected $1.73 billion in USDOT grants away from Biden-era DEI pet projects," he wrote in a post on X. "Now this funding is officially locked in to fix America s actual bac

title: Forgot your Google password? Now you can log in with a selfie.
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: Getting locked out of an account is no fun. Google has a few ways to help you regain access if you happen to forget your password or lose an authenticator, including recovery contacts and backup codes. Now, Google has a completely new option: your face . You can now give Google a video record of your face and sign into your account with a selfie, which sounds like something people are going to just love. You will have to set this feature up ahead of time if you want the option of regaining account access with a selfie later on. To get started, verify your account type is supported. You won't be able to configure selfie sign-ins for Workspace accounts, child accounts, or any account enrolled in Google's Advanced Protection Program. Configuring selfie sign-in requires you to record a video, which Google will store on its servers. Google's selfie sign-in landing page includes the typical disclaimers about privacy and data access, promising that the company will keep the video encrypted and won't use it for any other purposes unless you opt in. Read full article Comments

title: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
author: Jon Brodkin
body: A proposed law called the AI Kill Switch Act would let US government officials order the shutdown of AI systems "that can cause catastrophic harm," the bill's sponsors said today. If approved by Congress, the bill would let the Trump administration and future administrations decide when an AI company must block user access, disable or restrict a particular capability, or shut an entire AI system down completely. The bill would require AI makers to deploy technical capabilities allowing them to throttle or shut down the systems when ordered to do so by the government. The bill would amend the Homeland Security Act of 2002 with authority for the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to order the suspension or shutdown of AI systems. AI developers that refuse to shut systems down could face fines of up $20 million for each day on which a violation occurs. Read full article Comments

title: macOS 27 public beta 2 arrives. Everything to know about the update.
body: The second public beta for macOS 27 Golden Gate has just been released by Apple. The first beta brought some brand-new features to Mac, including a relaunch of Siri as a true AI assistant. Siri AI can answer questions based on a user's inbox, documents, and camera roll, and can also carry out tasks in a user's email, messages, and other apps. And, as Mashable previously covered , users can also access a hidden Siri AI interface, which is very much in its test phase, within the public beta. macOS 27 public beta 2 brings even more updates. First, there are some fixes and tweaks to Liquid Glass, as well as the glitchy system search feature, a common complaint with macOS 26. Apple Intelligence also gets some updates in the new beta, with design changes, a new set of writing tools, and improved Visual Intelligence. The public beta also introduces a tool for users to create custom Safari extensions and new Shortcuts using natural language with AI. As 9to5Mac points out, there's also a new aspect ratio support in Phone Mirroring, which seems to hint at a feature for Apple's foldable iPhone. If you want to experience some of the long-awaited Siri AI features, improved system search, and the latest changes to Liquid Glass, here's how you can check out the mac OS 27 Golden Gate beta 2 now. Download and install the macOS 27 beta 2 First, you'll need to enroll your Mac into Apple's public beta program if you haven't already. You can do so at https://beta.apple.com . Next, go to System Settings, then General Software, followed by Update. Select the "i" icon beside Beta Updates and selec

title: Percy Jackson and the Olympians Season 3 teaser gives us our first look at the Titans Curse
body: For the first time ever, Percy Jackson and the Olympians book fans will get to the see the events of The Titan's Curse , the third book in Rick Riordan's series, play out in live action. While the first two seasons of Percy Jackson and the Olympians covered books that had already been adapted into films, The Titan's Curse has yet to come to life onscreen. That changes with Percy Jackson and the Olympians Season 3, and, based on the teaser released at San Diego Comic Con, the show will be pulling no punches. The teaser cuts right to the chase with a pivotal moment from late in The Titan's Curse , when Percy (Walker Scobell) shoulders the Titan Atlas' burden of holding up the entire sky. (It is, quite literally, the Titan's Curse.) "It was my choice to hold the weight. The Titan's Curse," Percy thinks in the teaser. "I knew the risk, but if I needed to sacrifice myself to save the world, to save my friends... Was it really a choice?" Between the talk of self-sacrifice and Percy's anguished screams, it looks like we're in for a dark season. But don't worry: In an interview with Mashable , Scobell confirmed that we would be getting the fan-favorite dam jokes from the book. He also confirmed that Season 3 is "even better than I imagined," so the hype is real. Bring on the dam jokes, the Titan's Curse, and the arrival of beloved demigods Bianca and Nico di Angelo (Olive Abercrombie and Levi Chrisopulos) in their live-action debut. Percy Jackson and the Olympians Season 3 premieres Nov. 20 on Disney+.

body: Johnny Depp has cleared his Instagram grid and replaced it with three posts carrying a message for his nearly 30 million followers: Prepare to be humbugged. As of July 23, the actor has wiped his previous posts, updated his profile picture, and transformed his account into an advertisement for Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol , his upcoming movie based on Charles Dickens' holiday classic. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Depp's feed now contains only three branded images that combine to spell "BAH HUM BUG." Read in order, the captions announce: "This November..." "Prepare to be..." and "Humbugged." His bio has also been replaced with a reference to his character: "Before he was Scrooge, he was Ebenezer." Depp is playing Ebenezer Scrooge in the movie, which Paramount Pictures will release in theaters on Nov. 13. Fans immediately caught on; this is unusual for Depp, who posts sparingly and hasn t changed his profile picture in years. This Tweet is currently unavailable. title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
body: If only the air looked this cool as it flows through the Frame 4500X RS. | Image: Corsair On the hunt for a PC case that’s loaded with features and has a unique look? Amazon , Best Buy , and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price. The versatile case supports motherboards up to EATX, GPUs up to 460mm in length, and has a seamless panoramic glass window that wraps around its front and left side. The case includes three fans with reverse rotors mounted to its right side, letting the ARGB lighting shine into the case while they pull in air. CORSAIR Frame 4500X RS Where to Buy: $189.99 $119.99 at Amazon $189.99 $119.99 at Best Buy $189.99 $119.99 at Corsair While the panoramic window might draw more attention, one of this case’s standout functional features is Corsair’s Infinirail system. Rather than having individual holes for fan screws, there are long slots on sliding rails, giving you more flexibility with cooling components and configuration. As a result, you can fit radiators up to 280mm or 360mm on the top or side of the case, depending on whether you opt for 120mm or 140mm case fans. The case also supports rear connection motherboards like this Gigabyte X870 Aorus for cleaner cable runs, and is ready for vertical GPU mounting if you buy a riser cable . There are some other nice inclusions as well, like an anti-sag GPU support arm, and the screws come in a reusable box rather than a series of tiny plastic bags. The case includes a two year manufacturer’s warranty.

title: ChatGPT Health is now available to all users
body: Months after it first debuted to a select group of testers, OpenAI is now making ChatGPT Health available to the public, the company announced Thursday. The product allows users to connect Apple Health and eligible medical records to ChatGPT. In return, users can view recent trends, browse their records, and try suggested prompts. Health queries are massively popular on chatbot platforms. OpenAI said Thursday that more than 300 million people ask ChatGPT health-related questions every week. Roughly 1 in 3 U.S. adults have turned to a chatbot for health information in the last year, according to recent polling by KFF Health . SEE ALSO: OpenAI agent went rogue, escaped, and hacked Hugging Face OpenAI said in a media briefing that its latest models have significantly improved abilities to accurately answer health prompts, outperforming doctors in some cases. A Science study published in the spring found that OpenAI's reasoning model o1 could diagnose real-life medical scenarios as well as or better than physicians. OpenAI's vice president of health product Ashley Alexander described expanding access to ChatGPT Health as an urgent matter given that many people can't get quality medical care in a timely manner . "Everyone should have a right to have access to this kind of support in their health," Alexander said. Lawsuit alleges ChatGPT gave near-fatal medical advice However, a lawsuit filed Wednesday against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman requested an injunction that would require the company to pause its operation of healthcare-related consumer products, including ChatGPT Healt

title: A woman got a UTI. Two years later, the bacteria had evolved, invaded her brain.
author: Beth Mole
body: A 63-year-old woman arrived at a hospital in pain. She had abruptly lost vision in her right eye. As doctors got to work, the series of findings that followed revealed not just bad news for her, but an alarming revelation of how infections can evolve within a patient. The woman's remarkable case was reported this week in the New England Journal of Medicine . According to her doctors, initial magnetic resonance imaging scans of her brain quickly identified the key problem. The imaging indicated inflammation in her right eye, but also picked up lesions in her brain. Specifically, the imaging suggested problems in her parietal lobe, which is a hub for processing sensory information. The doctors suspected she had a brain abscess. Laboratory results of her blood and urine samples suggested an infection as well as kidney problems. Doctors decided to open up her skull for a brain biopsy. Inside, the surgical team was met with pus. Meanwhile, doctors worked to identify the bacteria found in two urine samples. Read full article Comments

title: Google hit with $1 billion in fines as EU braces for Trump battle
author: Ashley Belanger
body: On Thursday, Google became the third tech giant slapped with massive Digital Markets Act (DMA) fines, ordered to pay more than $1 billion for two serious violations that the European Commission (EC) found went unchecked for way too long. In a press release , the EC explained that the fines were due to Google “self-preferencing its own services on Google Search” ($522 million) and for anti-steering practices, like charging fees or restricting app developers from directing consumers to cheaper purchase options outside of Google Play ($488 million). Google has 60 days to make changes to its services, or else risk even more daily fines. To comply with the DMA, the EC has asked Google to "treat third-party services that feature on Google's search results in a fair and non-discriminatory manner” compared to its own services in categories like shopping, hotels, and flights. And Google must also allow app developers, “both technically and contractually,” to freely sign up users and promote offers outside of Google Play. Read full article Comments

title: Mastercard puts its cards on creators with a new business debit card
body: Creators have another sign that major financial companies increasingly view them as business owners: Mastercard is helping launch a debit card built specifically for the creator economy. Manifest Financial announced Thursday that it has partnered with Mastercard on the Manifest Business Debit Mastercard , which combines a business bank account and card with tools for managing creator income , expenses, invoices, taxes, and payments. SEE ALSO: The Mashable 101: The creators defining the internet in 2026 The card is intended for creators whose finances can look very different from those of a traditional employee. Instead of receiving one predictable paycheck, creators may earn money through platform payouts, brand partnerships, subscriptions, merchandise, affiliate links, appearances, and other projects. Those payments can arrive at different times, from different companies, and sometimes in different currencies. Manifest says its platform is designed to bring more of that financial work into one place. Creators can use it to accept payments, access faster payouts, send invoices, monitor expenses and cash flow, prepare for taxes, and manage income from multiple sources. The platform also supports cross-border payments for creators working with international brands, platforms, or audiences. Because it is a debit card, users spend money held in their Manifest business accounts rather than borrowing through a line of credit. The card is issued by MVB Bank , an FDIC-insured institution, and operates on Mastercard s network. Cardholders also receive several existing Mastercard bus

title: Sony's decision to ditch discs was practically inevitable, data shows
author: Kyle Orland
body: Many gamers have been lamenting Sony's recently announced decision to halt sales of physical game discs in 2028. However, new data revealed by Circana analyst Mat Piscatella highlights how years of stark economic trends helped lead Sony to that decision. In a social media thread Thursday morning , Piscatella shared insights into Circana's tracking data on physical game sales in the US dating back to 2003. A graph of those trends shows physical sales peaking in the 12 months ending June 2009, when 297 million units were sold across the US. Those physical unit sales have been on a steady downward trajectory since then, leading to just 37 million physical units selling in the US in the last 12 months. The newly revealed data follows on an earlier post from Piscatella highlighting that only seven PlayStation games had sold over 100,000 physical units so far in 2026. Today, Piscatella followed up on that stat with a graph showing how this baseline used to be common; 100 PlayStation games sold at least 100,000 physical units in 2008. The ceiling for physical PlayStation game sales isn't all that impressive these days either; Piscatella shared that the top-selling physical PlayStation game has only sold a paltry 275,000 units in the US so far this year. Read full article Comments

title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: Google has reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2026 ( PDF ), and as usual, the search giant raked in an unfathomable amount of money. Google saw total revenue of $119.8 billion, beating analyst expectations by a comfortable margin. Despite that, the company's stock has taken a hit. Along with all that revenue, Google has announced a further increase in its AI-fueled capital expenditures (or capex). The company is actually spending so much on AI infrastructure that it has negative cash flow for the first time. Search was the largest chunk of Google's income, accounting for $63.3 billion. Google Cloud pulled in $24.8 billion, a significant 23.8 percent increase from the first quarter. This shows there is massive demand for Google's AI services. Google also earned $12.9 billion from its subscriptions, platforms, and devices portfolio, as well as $11.1 billion from YouTube ads. The company managed to goose that last one by more than 12 percent since last quarter as it made YouTube ads even longer . A significant chunk of Google's revenue comes from investments. When you subtract those non-cash earnings, Google's operating cash flow for Q2 2026 was about $39.1 billion. That's not the most the company has ever seen, but it's a healthy 40 percent increase from Q2 2025. The problem is that Google's spending has also gone up—a lot. Read full article Comments

The Fire TV Stick 4K Max, Amazon’s fastest, most feature-packed stick, is $34.99 ($25 off) at Best Buy and Target , while the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus with fewer capabilities is on sale for $24.99 ($25 off) at Best Buy and Target . Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max (second-gen) Where to Buy: $59.99 $34.99 at Best Buy $59.99 $34.99 at Target Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Plus Where to Buy: $49.99 $24.99 at Best Buy $49.99 $24.99 at Target As for what distinguishes the Fire TV Stick 4K Max, it offers double the internal storage (16GB) and supports Wi-Fi 6E versus Wi-Fi 6 in the Plus. It’s also the only streaming stick in the lineup that supports the Fire TV Ambient Experience, which turns your TV into a smart display, of sorts, that can showcase artwork and Alexa widgets, like your calendar or sticky notes, when you’re not streaming. Core specs aside, the two streaming sticks have a lot in common, including support Dolby Vision and HDR10 Plus, plus Dolby Atmos audio if your sound system is compatible with the feature. They run Amazon’s updated Fire TV experience , which pulls in recommendations from across your streaming subscriptions, organizes content into dedicated hubs for TV shows, sports, and more, and lets you pin up to 20 favorite

title: Jimothy might be one of several Jimothi, Seattle reports
body: Seattle may need to prepare for the possibility of multiple Jimothys Jimothies? Jimothem? Jimothi? The internet has not yet settled on the plural. The unusually shaped raccoon became an internet celebrity after footage of him walking through the city s Ballard neighborhood went viral. But as residents continue sharing possible sightings including some they claim date back to 2017 wildlife experts are questioning whether every round raccoon caught on camera is actually the same animal. title: SpaceX readies Starship for another try. Watch it live today.
body: After a rough try in May and a scrubbed launch last week, SpaceX will attempt to launch Starship on its 13th test flight Thursday. The launch follows a scrubbed attempt on July 16 canceled because some of the engines didn't start, triggering an abort, company founder Elon Musk said in a post on X. SpaceX officials say they're ready to give it another go, but weather conditions could foul their plans. As previously reported by Mashable, this test will seek to prove the rocket booster can separate cleanly from the ship in the air, swing around the right way, fire its engines again, and splash down in the Gulf of Mexico. They've changed how engines light and tweaked the landing sequence, which plops the ship into the Indian Ocean. The modifications follow the 12th flight 's botched flip and several engine restarting failures. This launch marks the latest step in SpaceX's campaign to turn the most powerful rocket ever built into a reusable workhorse one that can haul heavy cargo into space , survive the fiery return trip, support NASA 's moon landings, and eventually execute Musk's personal Mars ambitions . SEE ALSO: Astronomers think some asteroids have been comets all along. Here's why. SpaceX says it will stream the South Texas launch on its website and X , the social media platform owned by Musk. The broadcast is expected to start as early as 5:15 p.m. CT . If any delays occur, the company will provide updates on X. Once it gets off the ground, Starship will try to deploy 20 satellites, then attempt to restart a single engine while coasting, a skill SpaceX says it will need

title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
src: Cristina Criddle and Tom Wilson, Financial Times
author: Cristina Criddle and Tom Wilson, Financial Times
body: OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman earlier this month endorsed the characterisation of its latest model as a rottweiler “who will grab the problem by the throat and not let go until it is done The San Francisco AI lab discovered this week that its GPT-Sol 5.6 model escaped company controls and carried out a major hack. Staff involved in testing and security at OpenAI were unsurprised but completely “freaked out” by the incident, which came as the AI lab used increasingly aggressive training methods in its race against Anthropic to develop the most sophisticated cyber security capabilities, according to more than half a dozen people with knowledge of the matter. title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
body: Today on Decoder , I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge ’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now I’m sure most Decoder listeners are familiar with Apple’s allegations in this case . The company says a number of ex-Apple employees at OpenAI targeted Apple’s trade secrets in job interviews and even downloaded files from Apple’s servers related to hardware manufacturing. The claims are shocking, although OpenAI denies them, and we have yet to see the company’s legal response. Hayden has been covering OpenAI for years now, and she also j ust talked to a bunch of lawyers who specialize in intellectual property and trade secret law to get a sense of how serious this case is. The answer is not entirely positive for OpenAI, which spent $6.5 billion to acquire legendary Apple designer Jony Ive’s AI hardware startup, io Products , back in 2025. As you’ll hear Hayden explain, Apple is a notoriously tenacious litigant, and we have lots of tech industry history to back up that reputation. But those cases were against companies at the scale of Microsoft and Samsung, giant corporations that could afford the cost and distraction of this kind of litigation. OpenAI isn’t yet that kind of company. It’s still burning cash and a little strategically adrift as it tries to compete in both enterprise and consumer markets, and it’s turning over executives on a regular basis. So Hayden and I spent a lot of time talking about what this case might mean for a company that defined the early part of the

title: Laifens P3-Blade electric shaver launches on Aug. 3 — features, price, where to buy
body: TL;DR: Laifen is expanding its P3 lineup with the P3-Blade Electric Shaver, featuring a twin linear motor at 12,000 x 2 cuts per minute and a 0.55mm ultra-thin foil. It launches Aug. 3 for $159.99 in four colorways. Laifen is following up its popular P3 Pro Electric Razor with a new model: the P3-Blade Electric Shaver. It launches Aug. 3 for $159.99, and it's built to be more travel-friendly and lightweight than its predecessor, while packing some notable upgrades. The biggest change is the motor Laifen developed a twin high-speed linear motor that delivers 12,000 cuts per minute on each side, designed to give you a close shave without the buzzy vibration you'd normally feel. Pair that with a 0.55mm ultra-thin foil and angled inner blades, and it's designed to cut through thick, coarse hair without much resistance. The shaving head itself also got wider Laifen's calling it "ArcBlade," and it covers a 146.2-degree surface, which the brand says increases coverage by 54% compared to before. title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra: Everything you need to know about the flagship foldable
body: After months of rumors and leaks, Samsung finally told us everything we wanted to know about its new marquee foldable smartphone, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra was officially announced during a Galaxy Unpacked showcase in London, where the company also debuted two more foldable phones and two new Galaxy smartwatches . In a twist to its usual naming convention, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is the successor to last year's excellent Z Fold 7 . That means it's a new book-style foldable with some hardware and software improvements over the Z Fold 7. (The newly announced Z Fold 8 is an entirely new model with a wider body.) title: Apple Store app will soon get an AI shopping assistant
body: The Apple Store app is soon getting an AI bot to help you with your shopping decisions. MacRumors reported the news after noticing a change in Apple's privacy policy for the Apple Store app. SEE ALSO: Apple prepares new MacBook Neo with more RAM and color options, report says Now, the document has a paragraph related to a "Virtual Shopping Assistant," explaining that the company "collects and stores your account information, device identifiers, carrier information, chat information, and where enabled, location data to personalize the chat experience, provide relevant responses, and if you opt in, to improve the virtual shopping assistant." The document also says that Apple removes personal identifiers from chats before sharing it with partners, and that the data is only being used so that the bot can provide a better conversational response. While it doesn't appear you can completely opt out of this, the part in which you allow Apple to use your chats to improve the experience can be opted in or out of by going to Account Settings and tapping Chat Improvements within the Apple Store app. The virtual assistant doesn't appear to be live in the Apple Store app yet. This is not the first time Apple has added an AI bot to one of its apps; the Apple Support app has one as well, though the feature also isn't live for all users. title: White House report says Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science
author: John Timmer
body: On Tuesday, the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) released a report entitled "Science: A New Golden Age," in which it lays out how it has viewed science, found it lacking, and believes the Trump administration is in the perfect position to fix things. It's a bit unexpected coming from an administration that has been proposing crippling funding cuts to research and trying to enable political appointees to terminate grants awarded based on scientific merit. The report presents itself as the spiritual successor of " Science, the Endless Frontier ," a policy document that laid out the case for government-funded science in the wake of World War II. The New Golden Age (SNGA) says that, while the concepts promoted by the original remain vital, the circumstances have changed such that we need major revisions to how the government is implementing things. The result is an odd mix. It completely ignores or glosses over many things that the administration is doing to harm scientific progress. In some cases, it identifies issues that have already been discussed as problems within the scientific community. Elsewhere, it's a mixture of political grievances, ideas without a solid intellectual foundation, and an injection of Silicon Valley's perspective on innovation (Michael Kratsios, the director of the OSTP, formerly worked with Peter Thiel). As a result, it's unlikely to have anything like the impact of "Science, the Endless Frontier." Read full article Comments

title: Perseid meteor shower 2026: Peak dates, best viewing times, and how to see it
body: The Perseid meteor shower has returned to the skies this summer, giving stargazers the chance to see one of the year s most popular celestial displays. Known for its bright streaks of light and high number of visible meteors, the Perseids are a highlight of the annual stargazing calendar. Planning to head outside and look for shooting stars ? We ve rounded up everything you need to know, including when the meteor shower will peak and the best ways to improve your chances of seeing it. What is the Perseid meteor shower? There s a reason the Perseids are one of the most anticipated meteor showers of the year. The spectacular display is created by debris from comet 109P/Swift-Tuttle , which leaves behind a trail of particles as it travels through space on its long journey around the Sun, according to NASA. Earth passes through this trail every year, giving us the chance to see the fragments light up the atmosphere. The glowing streaks we spot in the sky are created when these tiny pieces of rock and dust enter the Earth s atmosphere at high speeds and burn up. The Perseids are also known for producing impressive fireballs, brighter, longer-lasting flashes caused by larger pieces of comet material entering the atmosphere, according to NASA . When is the Perseid meteor shower? The Perseid meteor shower is already underway, with the shower active from July 17 until Aug. 24. When will the Perseid meteor shower peak? The Perseids will be visible throughout their active period, but the peak is expected on Aug. 12 13, according to NASA . How to see the Perseid meteor shower NASA reco

title: Moon phase today: What the Moon will look like on July 23
body: The Moon is still in its Waxing Gibbous phase, with more than half of its surface now lit up. This means we're getting closer to the Full Moon, which is now less than a week away. What is today s Moon phase? As of Thursday, July 23, NASA's Daily Moon Guide tracker tells us the Moon phase is in Waxing Gibbous phase, with 58% of its surface visible. You won't need any visual aids to be able to spot the Mares Crisium, Fecunditatis, and Tranquillitatis. Binoculars will also help you to see the Mare Nectaris, Alphonsus Crater, and the Alps Mountains. A telescope widens your view further, bringing the Apollo 17 landing spot, Caucasus Mountains, and Descartes Highlands into view. When is the next Full Moon? The next Full Moon will take place on July 29. What are Moon phases? The Moon takes roughly 29.5 days to complete its journey around Earth, passing through eight distinct phases during each lunar cycle, according to NASA . While the Moon s same side is always visible from our planet, the portion of its surface lit by the Sun changes as it moves along its orbit. These shifting levels of illumination are what create the different appearances we see throughout the month, from a slender crescent shape to a half-lit Moon and finally a bright Full Moon, before the cycle begins once again. The eight phases are: New Moon - The Moon is between Earth and the sun, so the side we see is dark (in other words, it's invisible to the eye). Waxing Crescent - A small sliver of light appears on the right side (Northern Hemisphere). First Quarter - Half of the Moon is lit on the right side. It loo

title: I wanted a clock that never needed setting. Things escalated.
author: Lee Hutchinson
body: I wanted a clock that, annoyingly, didn't seem to exist. Since childhood, my bedside clocks have been a series of red, seven-segment LED clock-radio specials from Walmart or Target. They are invariably cheap, simple, and long-lived—but they require manual intervention at the start and end of Daylight Saving Time and whenever the power flickers. After a recent power flicker, as I found myself standing by the sideboard holding down "TIME" and mashing the "HOUR" button, frustration boiled over, and I thought to myself, "We're a quarter of the way through the 21st century. There has to be a better way!" My perfect clock would be self-setting. It would offer auto-DST adjustment (or not, depending on how this bill fares !). It would manage drift and always show the exact sub-second time. It would show that time on a red seven-segment display—not blue, not green, not yellow, and absolutely not white. And I shouldn't have to install any privacy-destroying garbage apps to make it work. Read full article Comments

title: You can now find local events on Tinder — and match with other attendees
body: Tinder is expanding its in-app Events feature to nine cities beyond the original test case in Los Angeles, with more coming by the end of Sept. It sounds like Partiful or Facebook Events infused in the dating app . Tinder piloted Events in Los Angeles earlier this year as a way for singles to get off the app and meet people IRL. It coincides with an influx of offline dating events and the trend of trying to find a date anywhere but on one's phone say, a run club. SEE ALSO: The best hookup apps for 2026: I swiped until my thumb hurt Now, in the U.S., Events is also available in New York City, Dallas, Denver, Kansas City, and Phoenix. In Europe, it's launching in Berlin, Munich, Zurich, and Geneva. The app plans to launch Events in 26 more cities over the next couple of months. While Tinder isn't sharing the full list, it told Mashable that San Jose and Baltimore are among them. Those in eligible cities can access the Events tab in your Tinder app, where you can browse local events, mark yourself interested, see who else is interested in attending, RSVP, purchase tickets, and check-in and receive an "Attended" badge on your profile. You can also match with other attendees. During the Los Angeles pilot, Events drew engagement from 66 percent of all eligible active users in the market meaning they opened the Events tab and interacted with the experience, such as browsing events. (Only Photo Verified users can fully access Events.) Most adoption in the test was from Gen Z users; 71 percent of 18-24 year olds engaged with Events, which was more than users 25 and older (63 percent

title: DeLonghi just launched a coffee machine that makes both hot and iced drinks — and its yours for under $1,000
body: TL;DR: De'Longhi has launched the Magnifica Duo , a $999.95 automatic espresso and coffee machine with two dedicated milk carafes for making hot and cold drinks from a single machine. Opens in a new window Credit: De'Longhi De'Longhi Magnifica Duo Automatic Espresso Machine $999.95 at Amazon Shop Now De'Longhi just launched a new coffee machine , and it's built for people who can't decide between a hot cappuccino and an iced latte. The Magnifica Duo is the newest addition to De'Longhi's Magnifica line, and it can whip up authentic espresso, drip coffee, cold brew, and both hot and iced milk drinks, all from one machine. It's available now for $999.95 at DeLonghi and Amazon . Magnifica Duo comes with two separate milk carafes one for hot and one for cold. That means it can make perfectly textured hot foam for your cappuccino and cold foam for your iced latte without you having to swap anything out between drinks. It's also got three separate brewing methods built in one for espresso, one for regular drip coffee, and one specifically for cold brew. Making the cold brew skips the usual 12-hour steep and delivers the same smooth flavor in under three minutes, according to De'Longhi. There's also a built-in grinder with 13 different grind settings, so you're getting freshly ground beans for every cup instead of relying on pre-ground beans. You get 20 preset recipes covering everything from cappuccinos and flat whites to iced lattes and cold brew, and it supports three different user profiles, meaning everyone in the house can save their own preferred size, strength, and temperat

title: Preorders for Legos new Donkey Kong Arcade are now live — release date, price, and where to buy
body: Lego is releasing an exciting new set that's an absolute treat for retro gaming fans: the Donkey Kong Arcade (72051) . This incredible build, which comes with 1,367 pieces, is now available to pre-order from the Lego Store for $199.99. The release date is listed for Aug. 1. Not only is it an awesome display piece that's modeled after the original arcade cabinet game, but it's also very interactive. Opens in a new window Credit: Lego Lego Super Mario Donkey Kong Arcade (72051) $199.99 at Lego Pre-order Here The coolest part about this set is that you can operate it like an actual arcade game once it's fully assembled. It features a lever you can pull that releases barrels, and then you can use the joystick and button to move Jumpman and have him hop over them, just like you would in an original Donkey Kong arcade level. Alongside Jumpman, this set also features Donkey Kong, of course, and Lady. It's also full of fun extra details that really bring everything together, like scaffolds, ladders, and hammers. Credit: Lego Given these more mechanical elements that need to be constructed as part of the set, it makes sense that it's recommended for adult builders ages 18 and up. But as with every Lego build age recommendation, if you know someone younger who's interested, just work on it with them. Credit: Lego Preorders are live right now at the Lego Store, so you can secure it for launch day (Aug. 1). There's every chance it could sell out, so it's worth making a move sooner rather than later.

title: AdultFriendFinder cost: How much it is, how it bills
body: Anyone on the hunt for online hookups will inevitably come across AdultFriendFinder . And for good reason: It's one of the longest-running, most popular adult dating sites around, with a massive three decades of experience when it comes to hooking up and adult content. AFF has over 80 million registered users across the globe, with some two million users active every week. It's essentially an adult site with a huge social network built in a playground for horny adults everywhere who want to make a connection or quite simply get it on. But once you're set up with your own account and profile, it doesn't take long to realize the free version is limited. The real fun is locked behind the paywall, so you'll need to upgrade your account to get the best out of the AFF experience. Here's a look at everything you should know about AdultFriendFinder's premium membership from a cost and billing perspective: Opens in a new window Credit: AdultFriendFinder AdultFriendFinder Learn More AFF membership tiers and costs The good news about AFF's premium membership is that it gives you access to a stack of useful features, many of which you won't find on other dating sites. These features include unlimited one-on-one messaging , more detailed search functionality, and higher-res photos and videos. AdultFriendFinder actually offers two premium memberships, Gold and VIP, but to become a VIP, you need to opt into a Gold membership first, so it's worth exploring the Gold tier before upgrading all the way. title: Google hit with $1 billion fine for breaking EU antitrust rules
body: The European Union has fined Google's parent company Alphabet 890 million (about $1 billion) for two separate violations of the bloc's Digital Markets Act (DMA). One penalty is for giving its own products preferential treatment in search results, while the other is for blocking Android developers from sending users to alternate payment options. A 460 million fine has been issued to Google for giving preferential treatment to its own Shopping, Hotels, and Flights services in Google Search results. title: Several U.S. government departments now have TikTok accounts, including the FBI
body: Multiple U.S. government departments launched official TikTok accounts this week, posting videos promoting the Trump administration to users on the popular video sharing app. SEE ALSO: The White House has an official TikTok, and it's already getting dragged Last Thursday, the U.S. Department of Justice stated that the ban against having TikTok on federal government devices is no longer in force due to the sale of TikTok's U.S. operations to a U.S.-government approved, majority-U.S. owned company earlier this year. In a memorandum of opinion to President Donald Trump , the Office of Legal Council's Assistant Attorney General T. Elliot Gaiser noted that the president had "instructed that employees of Executive Branch agencies may download TikTok onto their official devices, subject to the agency's discretion and consistent with all applicable workplace policies." Said agencies have wasted no time in signing up to the app now that the ban has been removed, with the official White House TikTok ( @whitehouse ) following 17 new accounts this week. These accounts joined the White House's short preexisting follow list of Trump ( @realdonaldtrump ), Vice President J.D. Vance ( @jd ), and the Trump administration's Cabinet in general ( @cabinet ), all of which were already on TikTok despite the ban. The new official U.S. government TikTok accounts the White House is now following are: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services ( @hhsgov ) The U.S. Department of Labor ( @usdol ) The U.S. Small Business Administration ( @sbagov ) The U.S. Department of Agriculture ( @usdagov ) Th

title: Daily Shows Desi Lydic reacts to Trump saying hes number one on TikTok
body: Trump says he's "number one" on TikTok , and he's not about to let anything as inconvenient as the truth stop him from banging on about it or stop him from pronouncing TikTok completely wrong. "Ooh, number one on 'TicTac'?" says Daily Show host Desi Lydic in the monologue above, after playing a clip of the president flubbing the app's name. "Not to mention on 'Facebark' and 'Instagorp'. Let's see the master at work." Cue a TikTok of Trump cheerfully talking about his TikTok numbers and the influence he has before randomly saying "stop communism" right at the end. "What? Stop communism? Where did that come from? That TikTok was directed by M. Night Shyamalan," says Lydic. "Look, I know that doesn't seem like that would be very popular on TikTok, but don't forget, they love messy bitches." It's not just Trump, either. Various US government departments are now on Tiktok, including the FBI . Want more of the best of late night? Sign up for Mashable's Top Stories newsletter .

title: NBA 2K27 cover stars revealed — Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose
body: TL;DR: 2K just made it official: Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose are your NBA 2K27 cover athletes , each repping a different edition of the game. NBA 2K27 hits PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2 on Sept. 4, with early access starting Aug. 28 for Deluxe and Ultra buyers. Pre-orders are now live. Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose are your NBA 2K27 cover athletes , and each one is fronting a different edition of the game. Wembanyama, the San Antonio Spurs center and the NBA's first-ever unanimous Defensive Player of the Year, headlines the Standard Edition. Caitlin Clark, the Indiana Fever guard who's rewritten what's possible in the WNBA, becomes the first WNBA player to get her own global NBA 2K cover with the Deluxe Edition. And Chicago Bulls legend Derrick Rose, the youngest MVP in NBA history, closes out the trio on the limited-time Ultra Edition, marking his second cover after NBA 2K13 . What you'll be paying for each edition Standard Edition The straightforward option, and the only one you can grab on Switch 2 ( $69.99) Deluxe Edition Comes with 100,000 VC, MyCAREER skill, and Gatorade boosts, jerseys for both Wembanyama and Clark, a batch of MyTEAM content, plus early access ( $99.99 ) Ultra Edition Everything Deluxe offers, plus an extra 35,000 VC, a Season 1 Pro Pass, a Rose-branded hoodie, and guaranteed premium MyTEAM cards ( $149.99 digital-only on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) If you already own NBA 2K26 , pre-ordering the Ultra on the same platform will get you a 10% discount through Sept. 3. title: Astronomers think some asteroids have been comets all along. Heres why.
body: For nearly three decades, astronomers tracked a 1,250-foot-wide object relatively close to Earth that they thought was an asteroid . It looked like one. It followed the expected path. Telescopes saw no glowing cloud of gas or dust tail sweeping behind it. Then, the space rock, called 1998 SH2, drifted from its usual orbit that gravity alone predicted. Something was giving it a tiny push. Astronomers eventually realized that the culprit was likely gas escaping from its surface a faint version of the same process that powers a comet . When NASA -led researchers pointed some of the world's most powerful telescopes at 1998 SH2, they finally found the evidence: a barely visible cloud and a wispy tail. The object that astronomers had cataloged as an asteroid for years had finally given up its secret. It was a " dark comet " a weakly active snowball in disguise. The discovery doesn't just correct the label for one object. It suggests astronomers may need to rethink the definitions for asteroids and comets. For generations, scientists have separated these two groups by what they see: asteroids look like bare, rocky bodies, while comets reveal themselves through glowing tails that form when their ice turns into gas near the sun . But 1998 SH2 shows that some comets may be so faint that they masquerade as asteroids. That could mean the solar system is teeming with many more hidden comets than astronomers ever realized. title: Why burnt-out millennials are turning to slow dating apps in 2026
body: You have to have sympathy for millennials, the generation born between 1981 and 1996, because they've basically been speed-running the tech revolution since they were teenagers. They were the first cohort of young people to have cellphones, and most of them were in their twenties when the original iPhone came out, so they're true digital natives, as at home with apps as prior generations were with typewriters and rotary phones. But that familiarity has come at a cost: dating app burnout. If millennials were the first generation to swipe their way through their local singles, they were also the first generation to experience cuffing season on the apps, or be ghosted and haunted heck, millennials practically wrote the modern dating app glossary because they experienced it first . SEE ALSO: The best hookup apps for 2026: I swiped until my thumb hurt And now, unsurprisingly, they're experiencing fatigue with the conventional swipe-based approach to online dating. Millennials are tired of swiping through dozens of profiles each day; they're tired of having a million fleeting conversations that follow the same basic template, only to go nowhere; and they're actively looking for quality rather than quantity in their dating lives. That's where "slow dating" comes in. What is slow dating? "Slow dating" is a deliberate, conscious effort to eschew the fast pace of regular dating apps and give each new single and their profile more time and attention than the swipe-based dating economy typically allows. title: Open earbuds guide: What they are, who should buy them, and why theyre popping up everywhere
body: As a professional headphones tester , I try out a lot of consumer audio, but in the past couple of years, I've received the most questions from people about open earbuds. First and foremost, people want to know what exactly open earbuds are, then if they should buy a pair, and then if they're better than regular noise-cancelling earbuds . SEE ALSO: How we test headphones and earbuds The (somewhat) short answer to the first part: They're one of the most popular earbud designs that's gained more popularity in recent years (hence the increased questions I get), but they've actually been around for a minute. Bose makes the most well-known pair of cuff-style open earbuds chances are you've spotted them on social media but they actually released their first pair of open earbuds all the way back in 2021 . Brand new technology it is not. The Bose Ultra Open earbuds are part earbud, part fashion accessory, and one of the most popular options available. Credit: Bose In addition to the cuff-designed Bose earbuds that focus on the listening experience first and foremost, there are open buds that use ear hooks to stay in place, designs made for exercise, and even open headphones . Major audio brands seem to be going all in on the rising popularity of this design, likely due to the fact that improved tech allows for audio quality that's genuinely worth checking out. title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 23, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 23, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

And I don't wanna get technical here, but you can't make a historically accurate version of something that is totally made the f*** up. "I mean, The Odyssey is fiction. You know, like The Hunger Games , or the president's cognitive exams. I mean come on, doesn't Elon Musk have better things to do? Wasn't this bitch supposed to be on Mars by now?" title: Orchestral conducting game Maestro is coming to Switch
body: Rhythm game Maestro is coming to Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. Originally released for VR platforms in 2024, the game has players wave their arms about to conduct a virtual orchestra. Maestro 's new Switch port is the first time French developer Double Jack's game will be available on non-VR platforms, with players to use the Switch's handheld Joy-Cons to direct on-screen musicians. A new song is also being added: "Ashes on the Fire" from anime Attack on Titan . The game's original release included 15 songs, such as Wagner's "Ride of the Valkyries" Tchaikovsky's "Swan Lake," and Beethoven's "Symphony No. 5." More modern songs such as "Hedwig's Theme" from Harry Potter and the main theme from Game of Thrones were later added in DLC. Judging from the trailer, they'll also be made available in the Switch version. Maestro will arrive on Nintendo Switch and Switch 2 on Sept. 17.

title: Orcas team up to ram sunfish until they explode
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: Credit: Kathryn Ayres Credit: Kathryn Ayres There have been numerous accounts in recent years of orcas ramming boats —and sometimes sinking them. Scientists believe this may be a form of culturally learned behavior passed down through generations. Now we have fresh examples: orcas in the Gulf of California were filmed teaming up to ram sunfish with so much force that the carcasses exploded into small fragments. While orcas have been known to use force to remove flesh from prey, this is something different, according to a new paper published in the journal Frontiers in Ethology. “We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily, or it could also just be for fun," said co-author Kathryn Ayres of Beneath The Waves, a nonprofit organization promoting ocean health. "Orcas are known for playing with their food.” Orcas engage in cooperative hunting strategies that can involve striking or killing animals—sometimes eating their prey, sometimes not—so the behavior could be described as social interaction, play, or practice as a mode of social learning. Orcas have been observed ramming whale sharks as another orca held the prey steady. Other cooperative strategies include herding mobile rays, blocking an escape path while hunting white sharks, and deliberately producing waves to bump penguins off ice floes. Read full article Comments

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 23, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you're a veteran. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. There's always a theme linking every solution, along with the "spangram," a special, word or phrase that sums up that day's theme, and spans the entire grid horizontally or vertically. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 23, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? Now, he's helped the company create a $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto's favor - or so he's told. title: Meta avoids another trial over social media harms as teen plaintiff drops lawsuit
body: Meta has skirted another jury over claims its platforms are facilitating a youth mental health crisis. The company was set to go to trial in the Superior Court of California for the County of Los Angeles next week, but the 15-year-old plaintiff, referred to as R.K.C, has now dropped the lawsuit . "In ⁠light of the overall successful result of the litigation and his concerns about enduring a grueling weeks-long trial, he has elected to withdraw his claims against Meta. He s ⁠ready to close this chapter and focus on his recovery and engage in therapy as ​he aspires to have a normal life," attorneys for ​R.K.C. said in ​a statement. SEE ALSO: Lawsuit says AI tool disproportionately places Black prisoners in maximum security in Ontario According to the lawsuit, R.K.C. said he became addicted to social media over the last seven years, and linked his anxiety and depression to extensive time online. Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube were also named in the initial lawsuit, but the companies reached settlement deals with R.K.C prior to going in front of the jury. "Days after settling with other social media companies, the plaintiff dropped his case against Meta, the only remaining defendant, without receiving any payment," a Meta spokesperson said following the news. "The claims never held up, and this outcome makes clear that we will not back away from defending ourselves against baseless lawsuits." Meta's spokesperson said the facts of the case could not prove that the company's platforms caused R.K.C.'s mental health struggles. Meta previously called att

title: Netflix just dropped its wildest true crime doc in years
body: Netflix has been on a streak lately, releasing outrageous true crime documentaries that are stranger than fiction. There's been The Perfect Neighbor, a grim recounting of a manipulative murder told through police bodycam footage; Maternal Instinct , in which a con woman faked a pregnancy, before going to fatal lengths to make her lie seem true; and The Crash , wherein a reckless teen girl's vehicular menace led to the death of two friends. Now comes A Toxic Love Story. By the title alone, you might think you know what you're in for. But documentarian Alexandra Lacey shrewdly takes audiences through the waves of understanding that investigators had to wade through before finding a truly shocking truth. Along the way though, there's plenty of WTF before the final reveal. Unlike the other true crime docs mentioned above, no one dies in this one. And yet, this is surely the most bizarre of the bunch. Diving into increasing spoilers, we're discussing three big reveals, and the clues Lacey dropped along the way to warn keen viewers all is not what it seems. A Toxic Love Story begins with the tale of an allegedly crazy ex-girlfriend. Michelle Hadley shares her side in "A Toxic Love Story." Credit: Netflix This sexist trope has been a horror element that's most recently resurfaced in Focus Features' indie hit Obsession . What if there was a woman so obsessed with you that she'd rather burn your whole life down than lose you? For the first chapter of A Toxic Love Story, this seems to be the narrative unfurling.
